title: Masala Tofu Scramble
description: A spicy and high-protein Indian-style scramble using tofu. Perfect for a quick and hearty breakfast.
explanation: Think of this as a paneer bhurji-style breakfast built around tofu. The tofu gives structure and protein, while onion, tomato, chilli, and masala make it taste familiar.
The recipe is forgiving: use the vegetables and spices you have, then cook off excess moisture so the scramble stays flavorful instead of watery.
instructions: Heat oil in a pan and saute the onions and green chilli until onions are translucent.
Add tomatoes and cook until soft and mushy.
Stir in turmeric powder, garam masala, and salt. Cook for a minute.
Add the crumbled tofu and mix well so the spices coat the tofu evenly.
Cook for 3-4 minutes until the tofu is heated through.
Garnish with fresh coriander and serve hot with bread or chapati.
stepNotes: If you avoid onion, start with cumin, ginger, tomato, and hing instead. If you like more heat, add extra chilli or crushed pepper here.
Cook the tomato until it stops tasting raw. If tomatoes are not good that day, use a spoon of tomato puree or skip them and add lemon at the end.
This short spice bloom is what makes the tofu taste seasoned rather than plain. Add pav bhaji masala, kitchen king, or sambar powder if that is what you have.
Firm tofu gives a paneer-like bite; softer tofu gives a creamier scramble. Press watery tofu for a few minutes if it releases too much liquid.
Cook longer for a drier filling that works in wraps. Stop earlier if you want a softer breakfast bowl.
Serve with roti, toast, dosa, rice, or as a sandwich filling. Add salad or fruit if this is your main breakfast.

variationSections.title: Taste and texture variations
items: For a richer version, add a spoon of peanut curd, cashew paste, or unsweetened soy yogurt near the end.
For a drier wrap filling, cook until the tomato moisture evaporates and finish with lemon.
For a softer bhurji texture, crumble the tofu finer and add a splash of plant milk while cooking.

variationSections.title: Ingredient swaps
items: Tofu can be replaced with tempeh, soy chunks, or mashed chickpeas, but the protein and texture will change.
Onion can be replaced with cabbage, capsicum, grated carrot, or skipped for a no-onion version.
Fresh coriander can be replaced with kasuri methi, spring onion greens, mint, or a squeeze of lemon.
